rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VARTKES ANIVIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/23/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Attorney must give notice of the Amended petition to all required parties on the mandatory judicial council form DE-120 and include the remote appearance information that is required by the 10th amended general probate order found on the court's website. Notice cannot be given prior to the petition being filed and it appears the notice filed 2/22/2022 does not include the accurate title of the filed petition. 2) Please explain the starting date for the account. Page 1 does not state. Page 2 line 8 alleges it starts October 31, 2015. Page 5 line 6 alleges it begins November 1, 2015. The GC-400 alleges the account begins November 1, 2015. Property on Hand at start of account period alleges it begins October 31, 2015. 3) Please explain and provide the legal basis for the judicial council form GC-400 property on hand at beginning of account not matching the prior account property on hand at the end of the account. 4) Please explain and provide the legal basis for why First Rep. Account #2699 the ending balances of the prior account does not match the starting balances of this account. 5) Please explain the legal costs reimbursement for 5/26/20216. It appears $670.80 was paid to the attorney but receipts show only $573.30 in expenses. Attorney Vicken Sonentz appeared via courtcall for the 5/10/2016 hearing. Please explain why the estate should pay the costs of a roundtrip ticket and hotel reservation for the attorney when the attorney appeared via courtcall. 6) Please explain the legal costs reimbursed for 10/21/2016 and file the missing receipts. $200.21 was alleged for lodging no receipt filed and counsel appeared via court call. Please explain why the estate should pay the cost of lodging when the attorney appeared via courtcall. It is alleged the court call was $116.00 but the provided court call confirmation page which is not a receipt alleged the statewide fee for court call appears is $86.00. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/9/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE YELENA FRUMKIN SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. This matter will be called with PCN-11-295236. 1) The conservator's attorney's fees ($7,802.50) and costs ($1,798.14) approved by the Court's 04/12/2018 order were paid from the trust, even though the 04/12/2018 order required the conservator to pay those fees and costs from the conservatorship estate. 2) The trustee failed to allocate receipts and disbursements between principal and income in compliance with CRC 7.901(c) & Probate Code sections 16320 et seq. 3) The trustee's bond ($70,000) has not been increased. Based on the assets on hand ($80,918) and annual income ($54,072) of the trust, the bond must be increased to $149,000, including a mandatory statutory recovery cost. The trustee alleges that good cause exists to waive the requirement to increase the bond. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MYRIAM W. OLIVEN U/D MAY 29,2009 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/23/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain the legal basis for the account beginning 10/23/2020. Franza Giffen was confirmed as successor trustee on 08/12/2015 and there have been no prior accounts in this case. 2) Please explain the legal basis for attorney fees request beginning 06/19/20219. 3) Please explain the legal basis for the property on hand at beginning of the account being $1,402,227.17. The I&A's filed appear to total $1,470,230.17. 4) Please file a bank statement that shows the $100,000.00 transfer to conservatorship of Myriam W. Oliven. 5) Please explain and file a receipt for Dixie Miner - Care Management through 7/12/21. Notice of Conservatee's death alleges Conservatee died 06/26/2021. 6) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.34 D.] 7) Please file trustee timesheets that comply with LRSF 14.52. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/9/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Co-Trustees And Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine whether San Francisco is the proper venue. The petition alleged venue because the principal place of trust administration is in San Francisco. 01/07/2022 Memorandum alleged the trustee's principal place of business was 1000 Brannon Street, Suite 206, San Francisco California. 08/12/2021 Alla Reykhel died and 16061.7 Notice was not executed until 03/28/2022. The 16061.7 Notice alleges the principal place of administration is 868 Brannan St., Suite 307, San Francisco CA, 94103. Both trustees live in Hillsborough, CA. Attorney's address in Spokane Washington. 2) Court to determine how to proceed. Account number on page 10 of the trust is not readable and appears to be handwritten in when the entire trust is typed but appears to end in 8824. It appears page 5 of the First Amendment is missing. Second Amendment not provided. Third and Fourth Amendment do not appear to have assignments and only change the Beneficiary section 4.

Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.
